name: Clementine
first: Joint NASA-DoD lunar mission; first hints of water ice at lunar poles
description: Designed primarily as a Strategic Defense Initiative technology demonstrator, Clementine spent 10 weeks in lunar orbit returning the first systematic global topographic and multispectral map of the Moon. Bistatic radar measurements at the lunar south pole produced the first tantalising hints of water ice — confirmed years later by Lunar Prospector and Chandrayaan-1. The follow-on mission to flyby the asteroid Geographos failed when a thruster malfunction depleted the spacecraft's propellant.

events.label: LAUNCH
events.note: Titan II G from Vandenberg AFB.

events.label: MAPPING BEGIN
events.note: Systematic multispectral and topographic mapping starts.

events.label: DEPARTURE
events.note: Departs lunar orbit for Geographos asteroid flyby.

events.label: THRUSTER FAIL
events.note: Onboard thruster malfunction depletes propellant; asteroid encounter cancelled.
